turns.user_message: Things used to feel so effortless between us, but lately everything feels like walking on thin ice. I keep replaying our last few conversations in my head, wondering when the easy laughter turned into these careful, measured exchanges. I really thought we had a solid rhythm going, but suddenly I am second-guessing every little thing I say. It is exhausting trying to figure out what shifted so quickly when everything seemed perfectly fine just a few weeks ago.

Now I notice the same heavy silence settling in every time we try to actually talk. I catch myself holding back my real thoughts just to keep the peace, which only makes the distance between us feel wider. I keep hoping we will just snap back to how things were before, but the gap keeps growing with every passing day. It is frustrating because I am putting so much energy into reading the room, yet nothing seems to bridge the divide anymore.

I am completely drained from constantly tiptoeing around a connection that feels so fragile right now. Every interaction leaves me questioning if I am even recognized anymore, or if I am just a background presence in my own life. The weight of this sudden shift is pressing down on me until I can barely breathe, and I feel entirely untethered from the person I used to be around them. I just want the easy comfort back, but I do not know how to reach it when everything feels so permanently fractured.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens by contrasting with how things used to be. This just started recently.
